//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
//
// This pass implements the Bottom Up SLP vectorizer. It detects consecutive
// stores that can be put together into vector-stores. Next, it attempts to
// construct vector operations that can be put between the consecutive stores
// and the independent loads. This is an iterative process. The pass starts by
// finding all vectorizable stores and then builds up the graph of dependent
// instructions. If a valid graph can be constructed, the instructions in the
// graph are replaced by vector instructions.
//
//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//

namespace {

/// \brief Predicate for ordering of instructions.
struct InstCompare {
  bool operator()(const Instruction *LHS, const Instruction *RHS) const {
    if (LHS->getParent() != RHS->getParent())
      return LHS->getParent() < RHS->getParent();
    return LHS->comesBefore(RHS);
  }
};

/// \brief Helper class for checking if two instructions can be combined.
class SLPVectorizer {
public:
  /// Check if two cast instructions can be combined.
  /// This is the core fix for the miscompilation issue.
  static bool canCombineCastInstructions(const CastInst *CI1, const CastInst *CI2) {
    // Don't combine sext and zext as they have different semantics
    if (CI1->getOpcode() != CI2->getOpcode())
      return false;
    
    // Only allow combining casts of the same type
    return CI1->getSrcTy() == CI2->getSrcTy() && 
           CI1->getDestTy() == CI2->getDestTy();
  }
  
  /// Check if extractelement instructions can be safely combined.
  static bool canCombineExtractElements(const ExtractElementInst *EEI1, 
                                       const ExtractElementInst *EEI2) {
    // Check if the source vectors are compatible
    if (EEI1->getVectorOperandType() != EEI2->getVectorOperandType())
      return false;
    
    // Check if the indices are valid
    ConstantInt *Idx1 = dyn_cast<ConstantInt>(EEI1->getIndexOperand());
    ConstantInt *Idx2 = dyn_cast<ConstantInt>(EEI2->getIndexOperand());
    if (!Idx1 || !Idx2)
      return false;
      
    return true;
  }
};

} // end anonymous namespace

  struct TreeEntry {

  /// Check if a binary operation is effectively an identity (e.g., add 0)
  bool isIdentityOperation(Instruction *Inst) {
    if (!Inst || !isa<BinaryOperator>(Inst))
      return false;
      
    BinaryOperator *BO = cast<BinaryOperator>(Inst);
    if (BO->getOpcode() != Instruction::Add)
      return false;
      
    Value *Op0 = BO->getOperand(0);
    Value *Op1 = BO->getOperand(1);
    
    // If one operand is zero, this is effectively an identity operation
    if ((isa<ConstantInt>(Op0) && cast<ConstantInt>(Op0)->isZero()) ||
        (isa<ConstantInt>(Op1) && cast<ConstantInt>(Op1)->isZero())) {
      return true;
    }
    
    return false;
  }

    if (E->Scalars.empty())
      return nullptr;

      CastInst *CI = cast<CastInst>(E->Scalars[0]);
      Instruction::CastOps ExpectedOp = CI->getOpcode();

      for (unsigned i = 1; i < E->Scalars.size(); ++i) {
        CastInst *OtherCI = dyn_cast<CastInst>(E->Scalars[i]);
        if (!OtherCI || OtherCI->getOpcode() != ExpectedOp) {
          return nullptr; // Cannot vectorize mixed sext/zext
        }

      if (isIdentityOperation(FirstInst)) {
